what_it_does: It is the src-local AGENTS.md that documents coding conventions, runtime load order, the generative type system, and where different categories of code and registries belong in the src/ tree.
observations: React components use inline JSX transpiled by Babel in the browser with no build step.
Each <script type='text/babel'> gets its own scope; components share state/functions by attaching them to window.
Module-scope style objects must not be named `styles`; use component-specific names or inline styles.

A strict script load order is defined in `Counterpart Studio.html`, starting with tokens.js and surfaces.js, then decisions.js, kinds.js, memory.js, resolve.js, and other plain-JS globals before any Babel components.
The generative type system uses `MS_KINDS` (universal catalog), `MS_MEMORY` (learning/preferences/local embedding), and `MS_RESOLVE` (variable resolution into concrete instances).
Resolution precedence is explicit > preference > memory > ai > default, and required unresolved variables fail loudly.
The registries intentionally mirror tables in `db/schema.sql` such as `kind`, `memory`, `preference`, `variable_binding`, and `binding_source`.
`MS_SURFACES` provides semantic surface zoning; panels opt in via a `surface` prop rather than inline tints.
Shared primitives live in `components.jsx`; `app.jsx` handles screen routing and live accent/theme recompute; `shell.jsx` handles top bar and drawer navigation.
Authoring mandates tokens, CSS variables, and `MS_MOTION`; raw hex, px, and ms values are prohibited.
standouts: Browser-transpiled inline Babel with no build step and explicit window-global sharing.
A generative type system that resolves universal kinds into instances through a layered precedence chain.
A rigid, documented global load order that must be maintained in the host HTML file.
conventions: Extends root `CLAUDE.md` + `AGENTS.md` with only src-local additions.
Plain-JS global registries load before Babel React components.
Token, surface, kind, memory, and resolve registries are kept in dedicated files and mirrored in CSS/schema.
Style objects are named per component to avoid sibling-file collisions.
All visual/motion values are expressed through design tokens and CSS custom properties.
concerns: Window-global sharing introduces implicit coupling and collision risk beyond the `styles` rule.
No build step removes static analysis, linting, and type checking for JSX and imports.
Runtime correctness depends heavily on the exact script load order in a separate HTML file.
The `ai` resolution source is mentioned but not constrained or documented in this file.
notes: This is the src/ onboarding contract: it tells agents how to write no-build React code, how scope and load order work, and how the generative registries map to the schema and CSS.
questions: How are window-global name collisions prevented other than the `styles` prohibition?
What is the exact contract between `MS_MOTION._curves` and the accent/theme recompute in `app.jsx`?
How will the toy `embed()` evolve into the planned 384-dimensional local model?
Who maintains `Counterpart Studio.html` and how is load-order drift detected?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to add src-local conventions to the root guides; the content matches by detailing scope rules, load order, the generative system, and authoring rules.
apparent_themes: Design-system-first authoring via tokens and surfaces
intention_signals: It tries to keep a no-build React codebase coherent by centralizing scope rules, runtime load order, and a registry-based generative architecture that produces app parts from universal kinds instead of ad-hoc components.
novelty: The combination of inline browser Babel transpilation with a SQL-schema-mirrored generative type system that resolves instances through explicit>preference>memory>ai>default precedence.
contribution: It records the src/ conventions and runtime load-order contract that a fused system would need to preserve when extending the no-build React code and the generative registries.
summary_for_embedding: AGENTS.md for src/ documents browser-transpiled React conventions, per-script scope rules, a strict global load order, and a registry-based generative type system that resolves universal kinds into concrete instances.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to orient agents working in src/ on where to place code, how to share scope without a build step, and how to extend the generative registries consistently.
